2026-04-13T16:23:39.4498170Z   ### Body
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4498338Z   
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4498804Z   **Library name and version:** `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Files.Shares 12.3.0`
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4499178Z   
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4499339Z   **Query/Question:**
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4499542Z   
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4500122Z   > We are uploading large files (hundreds of MB to several GB) to an Azure File Share using the DataMovement v12 SDK. The file share is synced to a server via Azure File Sync.
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4500743Z   >
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4501671Z   > With the **legacy SDK** (`Microsoft.Azure.Storage.DataMovement v2`), Azure File Sync **never picked up the file during upload** — the synced file on the local server was always complete.
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4502362Z   >
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4503195Z   > After migrating to **`Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Files.Shares v12`**, Azure File Sync **picks up the file during the upload**, resulting in an **incomplete/corrupted file** on the local server.
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4504282Z   > Same test, same manual sync trigger, same Azure File Sync agent — the only change is the SDK version.
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4504742Z   
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4504941Z   **Environment:** *(No response)*
2026-04-13T16:23:39.4505191Z
